overview.description: Garfield is a diverse, working-class city in southeastern Bergen County, situated along the Passaic River across from the cities of Passaic and Clifton. Named after President James A. Garfield, the city has a rich industrial heritage and a large Polish-American, Italian-American, and Hispanic population. Garfield offers some of the most affordable housing in Bergen County, a gritty urban character, and convenient access to major highways and public transit.
highlights: Most affordable city in Bergen County for homebuyers
Strong Polish-American and Hispanic cultural heritage with authentic dining
Excellent highway access via Routes 17, 46, and nearby I-80
Passaic River waterfront with redevelopment potential
Active youth sports and recreation programs

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the City Tax Assessor. Garfield's housing stock includes single-family homes, two-family houses, multi-family buildings, and apartment complexes.
realEstate.recentTrends: Garfield remains one of the most affordable markets in Bergen County, attracting first-time buyers, investors in multi-family properties, and families seeking value. Prices have increased but still offer significant savings compared to towns just a few miles north. Redevelopment along the Passaic River corridor is anticipated.
neighborhoodNotes: The Outwater Lane and Passaic Street corridors form the main commercial areas with shops and restaurants
The western section near Route 17 has more commercial development and newer housing
The Passaic River waterfront area has potential for future redevelopment
The eastern neighborhoods feature compact residential streets with two-family homes popular with buyers
The area near Garfield High School has well-established residential streets

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection is provided twice weekly by the City's contracted hauler.
details: Collection days vary by neighborhood — check the city website
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use sealed bags or covered containers not exceeding 50 lbs
Bulk item pickup available by scheduling with the DPW

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Recycling is collected biweekly through the Bergen County Utilities Authority program.
details: Commingled recyclables (plastic, glass, cans) collected in blue bins
Paper and cardboard collected separately
No plastic bags, Styrofoam, or food-soiled items
Yard waste collected seasonally

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is supplied by the Passaic Valley Water Commission and sewer service is managed through the city and Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Water bills issued quarterly by Passaic Valley Water Commission
Sewer charges included in property tax or utility bills
Water quality reports published annually by PVWC
Report water emergencies to the DPW

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: The Garfield Building Department handles construction, renovation, and zoning permits.
details: Permits required for structural, electrical, plumbing, and HVAC work
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board or Zoning Board
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction and changes of use
Applications submitted at City Hall on Outwater Lane

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Garfield manages street parking and municipal lots throughout the city.
details: Street parking available on most residential streets
Municipal lots near the downtown commercial areas
Overnight parking restricted during snow emergencies
No parking on lawns or unpaved surfaces

additionalServices.title: Recreation Programs
additionalServices.description: The Garfield Recreation Department offers youth sports, camps, and community programs.
details: Youth baseball, football, soccer, basketball, and wrestling leagues
Summer recreation programs for children
Community pool at the Garfield Recreation Complex (seasonal)
Adult sports leagues and fitness programs

additionalServices.title: Senior Services
additionalServices.description: The Garfield Senior Center provides programs and services for older residents.
details: Daily activities, meals, and social programs
Transportation to medical appointments and shopping
Exercise classes and health screenings
Holiday parties and seasonal trips

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Garfield enforces noise restrictions to maintain quality of life in residential and commercial areas.
keyRules: Construction work prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 8:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or federal holidays
Excessive noise from residential properties subject to fines
Noise from bars and restaurants regulated after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties to city standards.
keyRules: Lawns must be kept trimmed and free of excessive weeds
Snow and ice must be cleared from sidewalks within 24 hours of storm ending
No accumulation of trash, debris, or abandoned vehicles
Building facades must be maintained in safe condition
Vacant and abandoned properties must be registered and secured

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Dogs must be licensed and controlled at all times within the city.
keyRules: All dogs must be licensed with the City Clerk
Dogs must be on a leash when off the owner's property
Owners must clean up after their pets
Rabies vaccination required for all dogs and cats
Pit bull and dangerous dog regulations enforced

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Garfield enforces parking regulations on city streets and public lots.
keyRules: No parking during snow emergencies on designated streets
Alternate-side parking enforced for street cleaning in some areas
Commercial vehicles restricted from parking on residential streets overnight
Fire hydrant clearance of 10 feet required
No parking on lawns or unpaved surfaces

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Garfield has a large rental population and enforces habitability and registration requirements.
keyRules: Landlords must register all rental units with the City
Certificate of Habitability required before tenant occupancy
Fire safety inspections required for multi-family buildings
Security deposit limited to 1.5 months' rent per NJ state law
Tenants protected under NJ Anti-Eviction Act
rentals.source: Garfield City Code, NJ Landlord-Tenant Law

other.title: Graffiti Abatement
other.summary: Garfield actively combats graffiti and requires prompt removal by property owners.
keyRules: Property owners must remove graffiti within 15 days of notification
City may abate graffiti and charge the property owner
Graffiti vandalism is a criminal offense
Rewards may be offered for information leading to vandal identification

publicSchools.name: Garfield High School
publicSchools.notes: Known as the 'Boilermakers'; strong wrestling and football programs; offers vocational and college prep tracks

publicSchools.name: Garfield Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Serves all city middle school students with bilingual programs

privateSchools.name: Our Lady of Sorrows School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school serving Garfield families with faith-based education

privateSchools.name: St. Stanislaus Kostka School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school with Polish heritage, serving the local Polish-American community

nearbyHigherEd: Passaic County Community College — Paterson (4 miles)

whyMoveHere: Most affordable housing in Bergen County — ideal for first-time buyers
Rich Polish-American and Hispanic cultural heritage with authentic dining and shops
Convenient highway access to Routes 17, 46, and nearby I-80
Multi-family homes offer rental income potential for investor-buyers
Active community with youth sports programs and neighborhood events

commuteToNYC.duration: 35-55 minutes by bus; 30-60 minutes by car
commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit bus routes run to the Port Authority Bus Terminal in Manhattan. Car commute via Route 17 or Route 46 to the George Washington Bridge, or via Route 3 to the Lincoln Tunnel. NJ Transit train service available from nearby Plauderville station on the Bergen County Line.

costOfLiving.summary: Garfield offers the lowest cost of living in Bergen County, with affordable home prices and moderate property taxes making it accessible for working families.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: At or slightly below the New Jersey average

pros: Most affordable housing in Bergen County
Rich ethnic dining scene — Polish, Italian, Mexican, and more
Strong community pride and active youth sports
cons: School ratings below Bergen County average
Some areas have higher crime rates than suburban Bergen County towns
Limited upscale dining and shopping within the city
Passaic River flood risk in low-lying areas

restaurants.name: Polanka Polish Restaurant
restaurants.description: Authentic Polish restaurant serving homemade pierogis, kielbasa, bigos, borscht, and other traditional Eastern European dishes.

restaurants.name: Taqueria Pueblo Viejo
restaurants.description: Authentic Mexican taqueria serving fresh tacos, burritos, tamales, and traditional Mexican specialties.

restaurants.name: Napoli's Pizza
restaurants.description: Long-standing Italian-American pizzeria known for thin-crust pizza, calzones, and hearty pasta dishes.

restaurants.name: Rudy's Deli
restaurants.description: Classic New Jersey deli famous for massive sandwiches, Polish cold cuts, and homestyle prepared foods.

restaurants.name: Garfield Diner
restaurants.description: Traditional New Jersey diner serving breakfast all day, lunch specials, and a full dinner menu at affordable prices.

parks.name: Columbus Park
parks.description: Garfield's main recreational park featuring ball fields, a playground, basketball courts, and a community pool complex.

parks.name: Dahnert's Lake Park
parks.description: Small lakeside park with fishing, picnic areas, and a scenic walking path around the lake.

parks.name: Lanza Field
parks.description: Youth sports complex with baseball diamonds, soccer fields, and community recreation facilities.

events.name: Garfield Day
events.description: Annual city-wide celebration with live music, food vendors, carnival rides, and community entertainment.

events.name: Independence Day Celebration
events.description: Fourth of July festivities with a parade, family activities, and fireworks display.

events.name: Polish Festival
events.description: Celebration of Garfield's Polish-American heritage with traditional food, polka music, and cultural activities.

attractions.name: Garfield Public Library
attractions.description: Community library offering programs, computer access, children's activities, and bilingual resources.

attractions.name: Passaic River Waterfront
attractions.description: The Passaic River runs along Garfield's southern border, offering opportunities for fishing and waterfront walks with views of neighboring communities.

attractions.name: Polish Cultural Scene
attractions.description: Garfield's vibrant Polish-American community is reflected in its delis, bakeries, churches, and cultural organizations along Passaic Street.

shoppingAreas: Outwater Lane — Main commercial corridor with shops, restaurants, and services
Passaic Street — Polish delis, bakeries, and specialty shops
Midland Avenue — Additional retail and dining options
Route 17 (nearby) — Big-box retail and shopping centers in Paramus and Rochelle Park

government.meetingSchedule: City Council meets on the second and fourth Tuesday of each month at 7:00 PM
government.meetingLocation: Garfield City Hall, 111 Outwater Lane, Garfield, NJ 07026
